chapter_title.ar: باب الْوَقْتِ الَّذِي تَبِينُ مِنْهُ الْمُطَلَّقَةُ وَ الَّذِي يَكُونُ فِيهِ الرَّجْعَةُ مَتَى يَجُوزُ لَهَا أَنْ تَتَزَوَّجَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 26 – The time which she becomes irrevocably divorced from him, and in which there happens to be the reversion, when is it allowed for her to re-marry?

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/149)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/149)
text: الْمُطَلَّقَةُ إِذَا رَأَتِ الدَّمَ مِنَ الْحَيْضَةِ الثَّالِثَةِ فَقَدْ بَانَتْ مِنْهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, and a number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Abu Nasr, both together, from Jameel Bin Darraj, from Zurara,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The divorced woman, when she sees the blood from her third menstruation, so she is irrevocably divorced from him’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr and A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn abu Nasr all from Jamil ibn Darraj from Zurarah who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When a divorced woman experiences blood discharge of the third Hayd (menses) she becomes stranger to him.’”
